# @zakkster/lite-gc-profiler v1.9.1

Zero-dependency GC and heap profiler that makes the zero-GC claim
falsifiable. Precise perf_hooks GC entries in node, heap-drop heuristic
in Chrome, accurate opt-in measureUserAgentSpecificMemory (`uasm`)
also in Chrome under cross-origin isolation, frame-anomaly fallback
elsewhere. Three-state gate verdicts (pass/fail/inconclusive), phases,
nesting regions, differential and rep-aware gating, per-op primitives
(measureOps/assertOps/compareOps) for hot-path gating, baselines with
fingerprinting, CLI with partial-report handling for hard exits,
node:test helper, explain mode via node:inspector heap sampling.
Single-file browser surface; node-only subpaths for register/
test-helpers/explain and the lite-gc-gate CLI.

## Entry points

- `@zakkster/lite-gc-profiler` (browser-safe, main): `GcProfiler`,
  `checkNoGc`, `assertNoGc`, `compareGc`, `assertCompare`,
  `aggregateGc`, `gateReps`, `assertReps`,
  `measureOps`, `checkOps`, `assertOps`,
  `compareOps`, `assertCompareOps`,
  `measureFrames`, `checkFrames`, `assertFrames`,
  `compareFrames`, `assertCompareFrames`,
  `measureOpsAsync`, `checkOpsAsync`, `assertOpsAsync`,
  `compareOpsAsync`, `assertCompareOpsAsync`,
  `aggregateWorkerReports`, `checkAggregateReport`, `assertAggregateReport`,
  `aggregateFrameReports`, `checkAggregateFramesReport`, `assertAggregateFramesReport`,
  `captureFingerprint`,
  `createBaseline`, `checkAgainstBaseline`, `assertAgainstBaseline`,
  `formatConsole`, `formatJson`, `formatMarkdown`,
  `formatGithubAnnotations`, `GcBudgetError`, `GcInconclusiveError`,
  `GC_DEFAULT_RULES`, `GC_DEFAULT_DIFFERENTIAL_RULES`,
  `REP_POLICY_DEFAULTS`, `VERDICT_MATRIX`, `VERSION`,
  `GC_MINOR`/`GC_MAJOR`/`GC_INCREMENTAL`/`GC_WEAKCB`.
- `@zakkster/lite-gc-profiler/register` (node): auto-attach preload.
- `@zakkster/lite-gc-profiler/test-helpers` (node): `withGcGate`,
  `measureGc`.
- `@zakkster/lite-gc-profiler/explain` (node + browser-safe formatters):
  `startExplainSampling`, `formatExplainConsole` (node-only heap sampler);
  `explainReport`, `explainDiff`, `gateBadge` (v1.6.0 evidence lane:
  pure gate-report formatters; browser-safe; no measurement, no
  observer, no perturbation).
- `bin/lite-gc-gate` (CLI).

## Key semantics

- Three-state verdict never silently green on unverifiable rules.
- Gates fail CLOSED (v1.5.1, extended v1.5.2). An unknown rule key throws
  `TypeError` rather than matching nothing; a non-finite or non-numeric
  threshold throws `RangeError` on the ops/frames lanes and yields
  `inconclusive` on the `checkNoGc` lane; a non-finite metric yields
  `inconclusive`, never `pass`. Do not pass rule keys a lane does not
  implement. Rule thresholds are read exactly once, so a getter cannot
  return one value to validation and another to the comparison.
- `uasm` is quantized and the quantum is not contractual (v1.9.0). Each
  window derives its own floor -- `summary.uasm.granularityBytes`, the
  smallest non-zero step between consecutive readings, or `null` when no
  step occurred (null means NOT MEASURED; never render or compare it as
  0). `summary.uasm.belowGranularity` is true when the window's net
  displacement sits inside one quantum or no floor was measured at all;
  `maxAllocRate` on `source: 'uasm'` then routes to `inconclusive` with
  `reason: 'uasm_below_granularity'` -- never pass, never fail. Same on
  `maxExtraAllocRate` (a delta is only as resolvable as its worse side)
  and on `gateReps` (ANY blind rep makes the set unresolvable).
  `growthRate` stays the RAW net/time figure and is not zeroed under the
  flag: the rate is the measurement, the flag is whether it resolves.
- Triage for the third verdict lives in `INCONCLUSIVE.md` (shipped).
  Reason codes: `source_mismatch`, `mixed_sources`, `fingerprint_mismatch`,
  `no_comparable_metrics`, `invalid_baseline`, `partial_report`,
  `uasm_below_granularity`. `GcInconclusiveError.message` names the cause
  and one next step; do not resolve an unexpected `inconclusive` by
  setting `allowInconclusive` -- that is the escape hatch, not the fix.
- One measurement at a time. Overlapping/nested `measureOps`,
  `measureFrames` or `measureOpsAsync` throws/rejects "already in
  flight" -- all lanes share one heap. Measure sequentially. The guard
  releases when a run settles; a run that NEVER settles (a scheduler
  that never fires, a promise that never resolves) holds it for the life
  of the process by design -- releasing on a timeout would resume the
  cross-contamination the guard prevents.
- `opts.capacity` must be a positive integer <= 2**24 (MAX_RING_CAPACITY)
  in every lane, and the GcProfiler constructor enforces the same ceiling
  (v1.5.2). Past it: RangeError. Before the ceiling, capacities above
  2**30 hung the process (32-bit shift wrap in pow2) and large ones below
  it silently allocated GB-scale rings.
- The overlapping-measurement guard is per module instance. Two copies
  of Gc.js (source + bundled) each keep their own counter and cannot see
  each other's in-flight runs. Import the profiler once.
- `record()` requires a finite durationMs >= 0 and a finite startTime
  when given; both throw RangeError otherwise (v1.5.2).
- start() and reset() are hard cutoffs (v1.5.2): 'gc' entries whose
  startTime precedes them are dropped even when node delivers the queued
  backlog of an earlier sync workload later in the same turn. A profiler
  never inherits GC history it did not observe; the synthetic record()
  API is exempt.
- Phases (linear, no nesting), regions (nested, LIFO enter/exit).
- Attribution is firing-site, not allocator. Explain mode answers the
  allocator question separately via node:inspector heap sampling.
- Rep policy defaults: majors/minors -> all-clean, pauses/rates ->
  best-clean. Override per rule.
- Baseline regression semantic: current.median > baseline.max. Both
  comparands must be finite; a metric whose baseline `max` is NaN/null/
  non-numeric reports `checked: false` (v1.5.2). A baseline with no
  comparable metrics at all -> `inconclusive` with
  `reason: 'no_comparable_metrics'`, never `pass`.
- Fingerprint mismatch -> inconclusive (D6).
- `summary.phases`/`summary.byRegion` define keys via defineProperty
  (v1.5.2), so a phase named `__proto__` is a real key. Prototype and
  shape are unchanged; no assertion style change is needed.
- `sampleHeap()` ignores non-finite readings rather than letting them
  poison subsequent allocation accounting (v1.5.2).
- CLI exit codes: 0 pass, 1 fail, 2 inconclusive, 3 infrastructure error.

## Zero-alloc guarantees

Hot-path methods (`record`, `sampleHeap`, `markFrame`, `phase`, `enter`,
`exit`) allocate nothing after the intern tables settle. Observer
callback increments one integer per batch beyond delivering entries.
Self-noise on old hardware: 0 majors, 2-4 minors, ~0.5 ms longest pause,
250-770 KB heap growth per 500ms noop window (mostly V8, not the
profiler).
